{"key_event":"Civil service reform and Crimean accountability","reasoning":"State power rose as meritocratic administration, compulsory professional policing, metropolitan/public-health governance, stronger fiscal tools, and Crown rule in India deepened administrative and coercive capacity, despite reduced mercantilist trade controls. Civil society also strengthened modestly through railways, telegraph, press scrutiny, free-trade commercial organization, and institutional reform politics, though the decline of Chartist mass pressure and weakened Irish rural society limited the gain.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":8.0,"state_change":0.3,"state_power":8.6}
